questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Toe the line
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} To follow a strict dress code
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} To stand on one foot while working
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} To adhere to rules or guidelines
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} To draw a line in the sand
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Toe the line" is c: To adhere to rules or guidelines Toe the line: accept the authority, policies, or principles of a particular group, especially unwillingly.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Chivalry
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate antonym of the given word "Chivalry" is: Rudeness Chivalry: It is characterized by qualities such as honor, bravery, courtesy, and gallantry towards women. Pluviophile: Someone who finds joy and peace of mind during rainy days. Scrumptious: Extremely delicious or appetizing; pleasing to the taste buds; often used to describe food that is exceptionally tasty or flavorful. Petrichor: The pleasant, earthy scent produced when rain falls on dry soil; it is caused by a combination of plant oils, bacterial spores, and other organic compounds released into the air. Rudeness: Behavior or actions that are impolite, disrespectful, or lacking in courtesy; the quality of being offensive or discourteous towards others.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. A manager and an employee were discuss the project.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence "A manager and an employee were discussing the project." is: Were discussing This is because "a manager" and "an employee" refer to two different individuals, necessitating the use of a plural verb.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. Was the bag packed by Kareena?
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Do Kareena pack the bag?
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Was Kareena pack the bag?
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Did Kareena pack the bag?
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Is Kareena packing the bag?
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The option that expresses the given sentence "Was the bag packed by Kareena?" in active voice is: Did Kareena pack the bag?

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Identify and correct the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the given sentence. Dharavi in Mumbai once had the dubious distinction of being Asia’s largest slum. Then, an embbarrassed Mumbai Municipal Corporation claimed Orangi in the Pakistani port city of Karachi has inherited that rather ignominious moniker.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The incorrectly spelt word in the given sentence is: Embbarrassed The correct spelling is: Embarrassed Dubious: Hesitating or doubting; not to be relied upon; suspect. Embarrassed: Feeling or showing a state of self-conscious confusion and distress. Inherited: Received (money, property, or a title) as an heir at the death of the previous holder. Ignominious: Deserving or causing public disgrace or shame.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Ignominious
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate synonym of the given word "Ignominious" is: Disgraceful Ignominious: Deserving or causing public disgrace or shame. Disgraceful: Bringing or deserving shame or disgrace. Praiseworthy: Deserving admiration or approval. Dignified: Having or showing a composed or serious manner that is worthy of respect. Respectable: Regarded by society as being proper, correct, and morally upright.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word from the sentence. Fragile April’s soaring temperatures also kept inflation in vegetables, particularly vulnerable as perishable produce, in double digits for a sixth straight month at 27.8%.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate synonym of the given word "fragile" from the sentence provided is: Vulnerable Fragile: Easily broken or damaged; delicate. Vulnerable: Susceptible to physical or emotional harm, attack, or damage. Soaring: Rising rapidly to a great height. Perishable: Likely to decay or spoil quickly if not preserved properly. Inflation: The rate at which the general level of prices for goods and services is rising, and subsequently, the purchasing power of currency is falling.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Students _______ submit their assignments by Friday at 5:00 PM.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate option to fill in the blank is: Must "Must" denotes compulsion or necessity. It indicates that there is a requirement or obligation for the action to be performed. "Would" is used to express a future action in certain contexts, but it doesn't convey compulsion or necessity. It is more commonly used in conditional or hypothetical statements. "Could" indicates possibility or ability, but it doesn't convey compulsion or necessity. It suggests that the action is optional or conditional. "Might" suggests a possibility or uncertainty, but it doesn't convey compulsion or necessity. It implies that the action is contingent upon certain conditions.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Rural consumers fared worse than their urban counterparts with both food and broader headline inflation reflecting faster price gains.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate antonym of the underlined word "counterparts" in the sentence is: Contrasts Counterparts: Persons or things that have the same function or role as others within a different context or situation. Contrasts: Differences or distinctions between two or more things. Equivalents: Persons or things that have the same value or function as others, typically within a different context or situation. Duplicates: Exact copies or replicas of something. Alternates: Other options or choices that serve as substitutes or replacements for something else.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. When an industrial town like Bhilai comes up in the midst of villages, some villages are totally uprooted while the lands of others are partially acquired. Q. but creates problems of ordering relationships between the native residents and the immigrants. R. The urban impact is to be seen in villages which are situated near an industrial town. S. The latter are found to receive an influx of immigrant workers, which not only stimulates a demand for houses and a market inside the village
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is b: RPSQ. Sentence R introduces the topic of the urban impact on villages: "The urban impact is to be seen in villages which are situated near an industrial town." This sets the context for discussing the effects of industrial towns on nearby villages. Sentence P gives a specific example of such an urban impact: "When an industrial town like Bhilai comes up in the midst of villages, some villages are totally uprooted while the lands of others are partially acquired." This follows logically from R by providing a specific instance of the urban impact mentioned. Sentence S continues by explaining the effects on the villages whose lands are partially acquired: "The latter are found to receive an influx of immigrant workers, which not only stimulates a demand for houses and a market inside the village." This sentence elaborates on the consequences for these villages. Sentence Q concludes by describing the challenges created by this situation: "but creates problems of ordering relationships between the native residents and the immigrants." This follows naturally from S, as it adds the resulting social challenges. Thus, the best sequence that creates a coherent and logical paragraph is: RPSQ

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The author portrayed the protagonist in her c̲o̲m̲p̲l̲e̲x̲ ̲p̲s̲y̲c̲h̲o̲l̲o̲g̲i̲c̲a̲l̲ ̲s̲t̲a̲t̲e̲.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence is: intricate mindset The underlined phrase "c̲o̲m̲p̲l̲e̲x̲ ̲p̲s̲y̲c̲h̲o̲l̲o̲g̲i̲c̲a̲l̲ ̲s̲t̲a̲t̲e̲" refers to a mental condition or state of mind that is intricate, pertaining to one's psychological well-being, thoughts, emotions, or mental processes. It suggests that the protagonist's mental state is not simple or straightforward, but rather characterized by complexity, depth, or intricacy. This could imply that the protagonist's emotions, thoughts, or behaviors are multifaceted, nuanced, or difficult to understand. Intricate mindset: A complex or detailed mental state or thought process. Varied mood: A range of different emotional states or feelings. Deep thought: Intense or profound thinking or contemplation. Broad perspective: A wide or comprehensive viewpoint that considers various aspects or angles.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Identify how you will ask everyone if the children were not playing the cricket in passive voice.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Was the cricket not being played by the children?
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Were the cricket not being played by the children?
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Was the children playing the cricket?
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Was the cricket being played by the children?
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option a. Rule: Active voice ----- passive voice Subject + was/were + not + V (ing) + object ----- Was/Were + object + being + V3 + by + subject Hence, “Was the cricket not being played by the children?” is correct answer.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank. Mr. Smith is known for his ___________(meticulous) attention to detail in his work.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate antonym of the word "meticulous" in the given sentence is: Untidy Meticulous: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise. Untidy: Not arranged neatly and in order; messy or disorganized. Thorough: Complete with regard to every detail; comprehensive or exhaustive. Diligent: Showing care and effort in one's work or duties; industrious and hardworking. Systematic: Characterized by a method or plan; carried out according to a systematic approach.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The committee's decision was a ratification of the proposed changes to the company's policy.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate antonym of the underlined word "ratification" in the given sentence is: rejection Ratification: The action of formally confirming or validating something, especially by giving it legal or official sanction. Rejection: The act of refusing to accept, consider, or acknowledge something. Denial: The action of refusing to grant or agree to something requested or desired. Veto: The power or right to reject or prohibit a proposed decision or course of action. Approval: The action of officially agreeing to or consenting to something.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. The meek is often /overlooked, but they possess/ great strength of character.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The meek is often
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} overlooked, but they possess
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} great strength of character
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The part that contains the error is: The meek is often The correct form should be: The meek are often The error in the given sentence lies in subject-verb agreement. "The meek" is a plural noun referring to a group of people characterized by their humility or submissiveness. In English grammar, plural subjects require plural verbs. However, in the original sentence, the singular verb "is" is used instead of the plural verb "are" to agree with the plural subject "the meek." The corrected sentence should read: "The meek are often overlooked, but they possess great strength of character."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following sentence, four words are underlined, out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Identify the INCORRECTLY spelt word. The pressure on food prices remains wide-ranging, with cereals the heaviest weight in the food category witnessing a 26 basis points qiuckening in inflation to 8.63%.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The incorrectly spelled word in the sentence is: qiuckening The correct spelling is: quickening "Quickening," which means to make or become faster or quicker. Pressure: Continuous physical force exerted on or against an object by something in contact with it. Cereals: Grasses cultivated for their edible seeds (such as wheat, rice, maize) or the seeds themselves. Witnessing: Observing or seeing (an event, typically a crime or accident) take place.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. We have a parliamentary and a legal system, a police and educational system built very much on the British model. Q. We drive on the left side of the road like the British. We have ‘bread-omlette’ and ‘cutlets’ as menu offered in many roadside eateries and canteens. R. A very popular manufacturer of biscuits, is actually named after Britain. Many school uniforms include neck-ties. S. We often admire the west and as often resent it. These are just some of the many and complex ways that British colonialism lives on in contemporary India.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer is (d) PQRS. Sentence P introduces the topic with specific examples of systems influenced by the British: "We have a parliamentary and a legal system, a police and educational system built very much on the British model." This sets the stage for discussing British influences in contemporary India. Sentence Q continues with additional examples: "We drive on the left side of the road like the British. We have ‘bread-omlette’ and ‘cutlets’ as menu offered in many roadside eateries and canteens." This expands on the British influences mentioned in P. Sentence R adds more specific examples: "A very popular manufacturer of biscuits is actually named after Britain. Many school uniforms include neck-ties." This provides further details and continues the list of British influences in everyday life. Sentence S concludes by summarizing the sentiment towards these influences: "We often admire the west and as often resent it. These are just some of the many and complex ways that British colonialism lives on in contemporary India." This wraps up the discussion by reflecting on the complex legacy of British colonialism. Thus, the best sequence that creates a coherent and logical paragraph is: PQRS

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who is excessively preoccupied with their own needs, desires, and interests, often at the expense of others.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words is: Narcissist Narcissist: A person who has an excessive interest in or admiration of themselves, often to the detriment of others' needs or feelings. Altruist: A person who has an unselfish concern for the welfare of others; someone who acts selflessly for the greater good. Philanthropist: A person who seeks to promote the welfare of others, especially through generous donations of money to good causes. Misogynist: A person who dislikes, despises, or is prejudiced against women.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Taking a bull by the horns
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Procrastinating on a task

questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Taking a bull by the horns" is: Confronting a difficult situation bravely

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following sentence, four words are underlined, out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Identify the INCORRECTLY spelt word. It should stay invested in Chabahar and seek to improve its trade and connectivity projects with Central Asia, which is esential for India’s continued rise.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} he incorrectly spelled word in the sentence is: Esential The correct spelling is "Essential." Invested: Having put money, time, or resources into something with the expectation of achieving a profit, benefit, or desired outcome. Essential: Absolutely necessary or extremely important; fundamental or indispensable. Connectivity: The state or quality of being connected or linked together, especially in terms of communication, transportation, or access. Projects: Planned or proposed endeavors or activities that are carried out to achieve a specific goal or objective.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Climate change is a pressing global issue characterized by alterations in temperature patterns, weather events, and environmental (1) _________. Human activities, (2) ________ burning fossil fuels and deforestation, contribute to the increase in greenhouse gases, leading to global warming. This phenomenon results in (3) ________ sea levels, extreme weather events, and disruptions to ecosystems and biodiversity. Addressing climate change (4) _________ concerted efforts to (5) _________ emissions, promote renewable energy sources, and adopt sustainable practices to mitigate its impacts. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1 is: Conditions In this sentence, we are discussing the various aspects that contribute to climate change. "Conditions" refers to the state of the environment, including factors like temperature, precipitation, humidity, and other environmental variables. These conditions are undergoing alterations due to climate change, making it the most suitable option to fill in the blank.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Climate change is a pressing global issue characterized by alterations in temperature patterns, weather events, and environmental (1) _________. Human activities, (2) ________ burning fossil fuels and deforestation, contribute to the increase in greenhouse gases, leading to global warming. This phenomenon results in (3) ________ sea levels, extreme weather events, and disruptions to ecosystems and biodiversity. Addressing climate change (4) _________ concerted efforts to (5) _________ emissions, promote renewable energy sources, and adopt sustainable practices to mitigate its impacts. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2 is: Such as In this context, "such as" is used to introduce specific examples of human activities contributing to climate change, such as burning fossil fuels and deforestation. It is commonly used to provide examples within a broader category, making it the most suitable option here.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Climate change is a pressing global issue characterized by alterations in temperature patterns, weather events, and environmental (1) _________. Human activities, (2) ________ burning fossil fuels and deforestation, contribute to the increase in greenhouse gases, leading to global warming. This phenomenon results in (3) ________ sea levels, extreme weather events, and disruptions to ecosystems and biodiversity. Addressing climate change (4) _________ concerted efforts to (5) _________ emissions, promote renewable energy sources, and adopt sustainable practices to mitigate its impacts. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 3 is: Rising The phrase "This phenomenon results in ________ sea levels" indicates that the sea levels are affected by climate change. The term that best describes the impact of climate change on sea levels is "rising," as it signifies an increase in sea levels due to factors like melting ice caps and thermal expansion of seawater caused by global warming.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Climate change is a pressing global issue characterized by alterations in temperature patterns, weather events, and environmental (1) _________. Human activities, (2) ________ burning fossil fuels and deforestation, contribute to the increase in greenhouse gases, leading to global warming. This phenomenon results in (3) ________ sea levels, extreme weather events, and disruptions to ecosystems and biodiversity. Addressing climate change (4) _________ concerted efforts to (5) _________ emissions, promote renewable energy sources, and adopt sustainable practices to mitigate its impacts. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 4 is: requires Explanation: In the context provided, the sentence structure indicates that "requires" is needed to express what is necessary to address climate change effectively. It conveys that addressing climate change necessitates concerted efforts.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Climate change is a pressing global issue characterized by alterations in temperature patterns, weather events, and environmental (1) _________. Human activities, (2) ________ burning fossil fuels and deforestation, contribute to the increase in greenhouse gases, leading to global warming. This phenomenon results in (3) ________ sea levels, extreme weather events, and disruptions to ecosystems and biodiversity. Addressing climate change (4) _________ concerted efforts to (5) _________ emissions, promote renewable energy sources, and adopt sustainable practices to mitigate its impacts. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.

It conveys that addressing climate change requires concerted efforts to reduce emissions.
